name: Butter Bean, Mushroom & Walnut Loaf
description: A simply prepared vegetarian centrepiece — deeply savoury, slices cleanly at the table. Fits the omega 3/6 strategy throughout.

ingredients.name: rolled oats
ingredients.notes: optional, if the mixture feels very wet

steps.title: Soak the porcini
steps.content: Place {0003} in a small bowl and cover with 150ml of just-boiled water. Set aside to soak for at least 20 minutes. Once soaked, drain and reserve the liquid — refrigerate or freeze it for use in a gravy another time. Roughly chop the porcini.

steps.title: Toast the walnuts
steps.content: Toast {0006} in a dry frying pan over medium heat until fragrant and just starting to colour. Set aside.

steps.title: Cook the onion and garlic
steps.content: Heat {0008} in a large frying pan over medium-low heat. Add {0004}, {0010} and {0011} and cook gently until the onion is completely soft. Add {0005} and cook for a further 2 minutes.

steps.title: Cook down the mushrooms
steps.content: Add {0002} and turn the heat up to medium-high. Cook, stirring occasionally, until all moisture has evaporated and the mushrooms are deeply coloured and almost dry. Add the chopped {0003} and {0009}. Season well with {0012}. Cook for 2 more minutes then set aside to cool slightly.

steps.title: Prepare the tin
steps.content: Preheat your oven to 200°C / 180°C fan. Line a 900g loaf tin with baking parchment and brush with {0013}.

steps.title: Make the filling
steps.content: Tip {0001} into a large bowl and roughly mash — mostly smooth but with some texture remaining. Add the mushroom mixture, the toasted {0006} and {0007}. Mix thoroughly. Taste and adjust seasoning. If the mixture feels very wet, stir in {0014}.

steps.title: Fill the tin
steps.content: Spoon the mixture into the prepared tin, pressing it down firmly and smoothing the top. A compact, even fill will help it slice cleanly.

steps.title: Roast
steps.content: Roast for 35–40 minutes until the top is firm and well coloured.

steps.title: Rest and serve
steps.content: Leave in the tin for 10 minutes before turning out — this is important for clean slices. Turn onto a board, remove the parchment, and slice with a sharp serrated knife.

notes:
**Porcini liquid:** Strain through a fine sieve and keep in the fridge for up to 3 days, or freeze. It makes an excellent base for a porcini and red wine miso gravy.

**Texture:** Don't over-mash the beans — some texture makes for a better slice and a more interesting bite alongside the walnut chunks.

**Getting ahead:** The filling can be made up to a day ahead and kept in the fridge. Spoon into the tin and roast when needed.
